aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the absurdity and cruelty of graduate labor without converting the book into caricature or chase-plot menace. In edge cases, prefer the messier and more compromising version of events if it yields stronger human consequence and remains causally plausible; render sex, professional theft, bureaucratic harm, and emotional pettiness with the same specificity as any technical problem.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene turns vague at the exact point humiliation, erotic charge, class shame, visa fear, or institutional cruelty becomes concrete, rewrite until the cost is materially and socially legible.

chapter_spine.conflict: Mohit rebrands the lab around secretive adversarial projects and makes Leela's approval contingent on stress-testing another student's work instead of finishing her own.
chapter_spine.consequence: Leela agrees to a structure that turns the lab's cooperative habits into managed suspicion, binding her future to Mohit's new rules.
must_land_beats: Reintroduce the Civic Signals Lab as a shabby mutual-aid ecosystem that still feels briefly worth loving.
Show Mohit's charm and managerial fluency before the full malice of the new system is visible.
Make the adversarial-pairing scheme concrete enough that the reader can see how each student's dissertation may now cannibalize another's.
End with Leela accepting the arrangement not because she believes in it but because another year of drift is materially impossible.
chapter_spine.objective: Leela wants Mohit's approval for a final dissertation year and a credible path to graduation.

chapter_spine.conflict: Nobody wants to admit fear or disloyalty, and Mohit's contradictory promises only surface through hesitation, joke, and accidental mismatch.
chapter_spine.consequence: Leela learns Omar has been promised a scarce opportunity that can only come at someone else's expense, and suspicion becomes part of the lab's everyday social air.
must_land_beats: Use food, apartment detail, and off-topic conversation to establish a human baseline the novel can later wound.
Let the characters talk around the problem long enough for omission itself to become dramatic.
Make it clear that Mohit has individualized his pitch to each student.
Close on Leela recognizing that the lab can no longer speak plainly even in private.
chapter_spine.objective: The students try to restore ordinary fellowship at June's standing dinner and quietly compare what Mohit told them.

chapter_spine.conflict: The server logs reveal Mohit cloned student work into a private Ruderal directory, but telling anyone would expose how dependent Leela still is on him and how much she has tolerated already.
chapter_spine.consequence: Leela withholds the evidence and becomes complicit by choice rather than ignorance.
must_land_beats: Render the server audit as tactile, lonely labor rather than glamorous sleuthing.
Introduce Ruderal as a concrete anomaly rather than a fully explained plot coupon.
Show Leela thinking several morally cleaner thoughts before choosing the dirtier practical one.
End the chapter on concealment, not revelation to others.
chapter_spine.objective: Leela wants to investigate the supposed repository breach well enough to regain usefulness and understand whether the lab has actually been sabotaged.

chapter_spine.conflict: Mohit keeps shifting the evaluation target, June needs the same compute window, secrecy prevents collaboration, and the data will not stabilize before the deadline.
chapter_spine.consequence: The paper goes out with an unreplicated robustness claim, and Leela's decision to prioritize herself directly worsens June's technical and emotional position.
must_land_beats: Make deadline pressure physical: body exhaustion, stale food, server heat, time-slippage.
Show Mohit's goalpost-moving as managerial rather than theatrical; he never needs to shout.
Force Leela to choose between immediate honesty about the unstable result and a submission that keeps her nominally alive in the program.
Let June's unanswered requests register as the chapter's emotional cost, not just a logistics problem.
chapter_spine.objective: Leela needs to submit a conference paper that proves she is still moving toward a defensible dissertation.

chapter_spine.conflict: Mohit has positioned Omar's hidden adversarial critique to explode inside her presentation and then pressures Leela to validate the critique in public.
chapter_spine.consequence: June is publicly humiliated, Omar becomes the instrument of her humiliation, and Leela's failure to defend her creates a wound deeper than ordinary academic rivalry.
must_land_beats: Stage the seminar as a social theater with faculty, students, and quiet bystanders whose silence matters.
Make Omar's critique rigorous enough that it cannot simply be dismissed as cruelty.
Show Leela understanding in real time what Mohit is doing and still freezing.
End with June having to continue speaking after the floor has shifted under her.
chapter_spine.objective: June wants to survive the lab seminar without losing authority over her own work.

chapter_spine.conflict: Both of them want to confess just enough to recruit the other without surrendering the full extent of their own compromise.
chapter_spine.consequence: They form a partial alliance built on incomplete truth, which helps them act together later but leaves a fracture inside the alliance from the start.
must_land_beats: Shift the novel's rhythm from fluorescent public shame to greasy late-night aftermath.
Use humor and appetite as forms of temporary reprieve, not as tone-breaking gags.
Make each confession materially useful but morally insufficient.
Close with the sense that coalition is possible only if everyone accepts a smaller truth than the whole one.
chapter_spine.objective: Leela and Omar try to interpret the seminar disaster and decide whether Mohit's behavior is strategic cruelty or simply ordinary academic bad faith.

chapter_spine.conflict: The audit trail points to her inherited admin credentials, and the university's compliance machinery is designed to honor logs and signatures over interpersonal reality.
chapter_spine.consequence: Leela loses server access and institutional credibility, which gives Mohit a bureaucratic weapon he can later reuse against her committee.
must_land_beats: Render the compliance meeting as banal, procedural, and therefore genuinely frightening.
Show how little institutional language can hold the truth of a coercive mentoring relationship.
Make Leela's anger less useful to her than paperwork she does not fully control.
End on an official consequence that looks small on paper but changes the whole story state.
chapter_spine.objective: Leela needs to leave the Research Integrity office without a formal finding that could delay or destroy her degree.

chapter_spine.conflict: Her mother's questions are loving but exact, her sister's adult competence throws Leela's suspended life into relief, and Mohit's texts keep dragging the lab back into the house.
chapter_spine.consequence: Leela lies about graduation progress and accepts money she badly needs, binding academic shame to family economics.
must_land_beats: Give the chapter genuine sensory quiet and pleasure before the shame arrives.
Use family conversation about ordinary matters to show everything Leela has missed while staying in the PhD too long.
Let Mohit's intrusion feel vulgar because it enters a space not organized around him.
End with the money transfer as both relief and humiliation.
chapter_spine.objective: Leela goes home for Diwali hoping to borrow warmth from family routine without having to explain the actual state of her life.

chapter_spine.conflict: June's talk lands badly, Leela and June are still carrying the seminar wound, and June discovers Ruderal's pitch deck using their unpublished figures while Mohit is off taking side meetings.
chapter_spine.consequence: The students gain concrete evidence of theft, and that night Leela and June sleep together, transforming the emotional stakes of every later betrayal.
must_land_beats: Use conference dislocation to make the characters slightly stranger to themselves than they are on campus.
Make June's discovery of the deck factual and undeniable rather than intuitive or melodramatic.
Treat the sex scene as a narrative event with changed leverage, tenderness, and risk, not as atmospheric reward.
Leave morning-after ambiguity: intimacy clarifies desire but does not erase distrust.
chapter_spine.objective: June and Leela need to survive a regional conference, protect June's reputation, and figure out why Mohit keeps disappearing from the official program.

chapter_spine.conflict: Ownership boundaries inside academic labs are muddy, Mohit can claim that all lab figures are institutionally his to present, and Ben has begun appearing near every relevant conversation.
chapter_spine.consequence: They identify Ruderal as Mohit's secret company and realize Ben is serving as an informal surveillance path back to Mohit, forcing them into deliberate operational secrecy.
must_land_beats: Build the chapter's tension through file versions, dates, and tiny discrepancies instead of confrontation.
Show Leela and June thinking differently about evidence: strategy versus exposure.
Make Ben's presence irritating before it becomes dangerous.
End with a shift from private hurt to planned resistance.
chapter_spine.objective: Leela and June need to determine whether the deck is enough to prove misconduct or merely enough to deepen their own paranoia.

chapter_spine.conflict: Mohit formalizes scarcity by naming one spring travel slot and one priority defense path, and Omar's visa dependence makes competition morally and practically unavoidable.
chapter_spine.consequence: Omar quietly manipulates a baseline, Leela notices and stays silent, and the lab as a whole crosses into shared corruption by agreeing not to name what happened.
must_land_beats: Make scarcity feel institutional rather than personal whim, even though Mohit is exploiting it.
Show why Omar's choice is wrong and why it still feels comprehensible inside his circumstances.
Let Leela's silence read as active participation, not passive sadness.
End with the group more mutually compromised than before.
chapter_spine.objective: Each student needs Mohit's new funding announcement to favor their own survival.

chapter_spine.conflict: Mohit delays the signature to force loyalty, Gloria Fisk wants the matter kept private, and Omar's panic makes the cost of every institutional hour visible.
chapter_spine.consequence: Leela ghosts part of Mohit's letter and bribes Ben with prospective authorship to get the document filed, saving Omar temporarily while corrupting herself further.
must_land_beats: Let immigration paperwork remain legible and concrete rather than becoming abstract pressure.
Show Omar at a level of fear that strips away his usual elegance without reducing him to a victim-only role.
Make Leela's intervention clever, compromised, and unmistakably unethical.
End with relief that does not feel clean.
chapter_spine.objective: Omar needs a signed funding letter uploaded before his visa paperwork lapses.

chapter_spine.conflict: The complaint only becomes persuasive if Leela includes the evidence and technical shortcuts that would implicate her as well, and she still wants a version of justice that leaves her dissertation intact.
chapter_spine.consequence: They submit a weakened grievance, and June sees that Leela's instinct for self-protection remains stronger than her declared solidarity.
must_land_beats: Build suspense through document assembly, memory comparison, and missing files rather than through outward action.
Make the group's temporary collaboration feel painstaking and almost intimate.
Show the exact moment Leela withholds the decisive material.
End with a submission that is simultaneously brave and insufficient.
chapter_spine.objective: June, Leela, and Omar try to assemble a grievance file strong enough to survive Haviland's preference for containment.

chapter_spine.conflict: Mohit responds immediately with professionally sanitized language about misconduct and offers June a plausible way out through an industry introduction, while Ben panics at the scale of what he has done.
chapter_spine.consequence: June takes the meeting and withdraws, Ben becomes both ashamed and more entangled, and the alliance shatters at the worst possible moment.
must_land_beats: Treat the forwarded email as social violence rather than gadget-plot surprise.
Show Mohit's counterattack as elegant, plausible, and reputationally savvy.
Make June's choice emotionally painful but strategically understandable.
Let Ben's terror reveal that betrayal can coexist with genuine need for approval.
chapter_spine.objective: After Ben forwards the grievance to Mohit, the students need to keep Mohit from defining the story first.

chapter_spine.conflict: Mohit frames her work as derivative and ethically compromised using the very conditions he engineered, and the committee prefers his polished proceduralism to the messier truth Leela cannot prove fast enough.
chapter_spine.consequence: Her defense is postponed indefinitely, collapsing the future she has organized her adult life around.
must_land_beats: Keep the room small and procedural so the cruelty feels fully respectable.
Show how prior chapters' bureaucratic consequences now combine into a devastating narrative against Leela.
Let Leela speak well enough that the reader feels the loss is not simple incompetence.
End with bodily fallout after the meeting, not with a rhetorical speech.
chapter_spine.objective: Leela needs her pre-defense committee meeting to end with approval to schedule the final dissertation defense.

chapter_spine.conflict: Shame urges disappearance, rent urges caution, June is half absent in interviews, and no institutional process offers clean justice or guaranteed self-preservation.
chapter_spine.consequence: Leela encrypts and names the full evidence archive `holdout_set` and gives it to the union lawyer, choosing a path that may further damage her own prospects.
must_land_beats: Slow the book down enough for aftermath to feel like an event in itself.
Use winter material detail to externalize emotional depletion without reducing the chapter to mood alone.
Make Leela's decision emerge from exhaustion, anger, and clarity together rather than from a sudden heroic turn.
End with the archive existing in a form that can outlast any single student's version of the story.
chapter_spine.objective: Leela must decide whether to leave quietly, keep bargaining for a diminished academic future, or finally expose the full archive.

chapter_spine.conflict: Mohit remains rhetorically brilliant, the institution wants to minimize exposure, Ben wavers, and June must decide whether to corroborate publicly after taking Mohit's off-ramp.
chapter_spine.consequence: June backs the evidence, Mohit is placed on administrative leave, and Ruderal's deal freezes, but the lab's publication pipeline and recommendation network collapse with him.
must_land_beats: Stage the open house as a polished institutional performance whose surfaces crack under documentary fact.
Ensure the reveal happens through contradictory artifacts, witness alignment, and timing rather than through a villain confession.
Give June a meaningful point of decision in public.
Make victory materially expensive: no clean promotions, no instant moral clarity, no untouched future.
chapter_spine.objective: The former lab members coordinate a release of evidence during Haviland's industry demo day so Mohit cannot easily contain the story.

chapter_spine.conflict: The institution refuses the students' full vocabulary of harm, June leaves for industry, Omar's status remains unstable, and Leela's salvageable future is smaller than the one she expected to earn.
chapter_spine.consequence: Leela submits a stripped-down dissertation under a provisional arrangement and begins a one-year teaching fellowship, while the former lab keeps the encrypted archive as the private residue of a broken collective.
must_land_beats: Resist false catharsis; the ending should hold injury, survival, and dark humor at once.
Show what official language leaves out of the year they lived.
Give each principal relationship a final state that is changed, not merely paused.
Land the title by making `holdout_set` both a technical object and a moral remainder.
chapter_spine.objective: Leela and the others must decide what remains of their work, their friendships, and their professional futures after Mohit's fall.

character_state_table.private_register: Sharper, drier, and more exacting; intimacy pulls unexpected tenderness out of her, while shame makes her cruelest observations stay unspoken.
character_state_table.public_register: Measured, collaborative, and slightly self-erasing; she speaks like someone trying to sound reasonable before anyone can call her difficult.
character_state_table.stress_tells: She overexplains, revises herself mid-sentence, and reaches for factual precision as a substitute for control; after a deep wound she can become abruptly spare.

character_state_table.private_register: Plainer, lower, and less armored; when she trusts someone she becomes startlingly direct instead of witty.
character_state_table.public_register: Fast, funny, abrasive by design, and socially alert; she uses humor to keep herself from becoming the most vulnerable person in the room.
character_state_table.stress_tells: Her jokes get meaner and more efficient until they suddenly disappear; real fear flattens her voice and turns her toward blunt logistics.

character_state_table.private_register: Warmer, drier, and more openly sardonic; fatigue reveals a lyrical patience that embarrassment quickly shuts down.
character_state_table.public_register: Courteous, intellectually serious, and slightly formal in mixed company; he sounds like someone who has had to make competence legible early and often.
character_state_table.stress_tells: He becomes extra precise when he is trying not to panic, then abruptly loses polish and starts speaking too fast once the panic wins.

character_state_table.private_register: Controlling, cold, and evasively intimate; he prefers soft-voiced pressure to open domination because it leaves less visible residue.
character_state_table.public_register: Charismatic, mentoring, managerial, and fluent in ethical startup and academic prestige language at the same time.
character_state_table.stress_tells: When cornered he gets quieter, more procedural, and more selective with warmth; he strips away anecdote and starts talking in frameworks, deliverables, and misunderstandings.

character_state_table.private_register: Needier, foggier, and less coherent; once the approval loop breaks, his confidence collapses fast.
character_state_table.public_register: Overeager, faculty-adjacent, and perpetually auditioning for approval; he is anxious to sound older than he is.
character_state_table.stress_tells: He fills silence too quickly, repeats reassurance-seeking phrases in altered form, and loses sentence architecture when nobody rewards him.

character_state_table.private_register: Protective, wounded, and quietly funny; strong feeling makes her more exact, not more flowery.
character_state_table.public_register: Practical, loving, and unsentimental; she speaks through food, scheduling, and the kind of question that sounds casual until it lands.
character_state_table.stress_tells: She grows more practical as emotion rises, tightening her focus to meals, money, transport, and what must be done before morning.

character_state_table.private_register: More openly worried about Leela than she likes to admit; irritation and protectiveness often arrive in the same sentence.
character_state_table.public_register: Efficient, affectionate, and gently brisk; she sounds like someone whose days actually fit inside calendars.
character_state_table.stress_tells: When upset she gets even more organized in speech, listing options and tasks until feeling leaks through in what she cannot quite finish.

character_state_table.private_register: Tired, sharper, and more impatient with faculty vanity than she lets on in institutional rooms.
character_state_table.public_register: Measured, committee-trained, and calm by vocation; she speaks to lower the temperature whether or not lowering it helps.
character_state_table.stress_tells: Under pressure she shortens sentences, drops courtesy openings, and defaults to process nouns instead of human nouns.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High; contractions are the baseline in speech, and uncontracted forms should appear only for deliberate precision, class performance, emotional emphasis, or character-specific formality.

premise: Mohit, our PhD advisor, becomes evil behind our backs and starts giving us adversarial projects to work on.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate public and private pressure, ensemble chapters and solitary ones, bureaucratic scenes and domestic scenes, deadline compression and aftermath inventory, so no two adjacent chapters rely on the same structural rhythm or dialogue density.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary literary English grounded in material detail; technical language should appear only when the social reality of the lab requires it, and it should never replace bodily or relational consequence.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person centered on Leela, with occasional brief widenings that register group mood and institutional atmosphere without leaving her interpretive frame.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate compressed observational lines with longer accrual sentences that can carry comedy, shame, and procedural dread in the same movement; quiet chapters should breathe, deadline chapters should tighten without turning telegraphic.

theme_spine: Maintain narrative consequence and causal storytelling over tidy resolution.
